title: Compare Using Bar Models
essential_question: How can a bar model help solve comparison problems?

pretest.question: Sam has 245 stickers. Ella has 178 more than Sam. How many does Ella have?

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is D. 245+178=423.

hints: Read the problem twice. Identify what's given and what's asked, then choose an operation.

pretest.question: Tom has $350. Lee has $215 less than Tom. How much does Lee have?
hints: Subtract: 350−215.
Try drawing a picture or using a place-value chart.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is 135. Work through it place by place.

pretest.question: A book has 250 pages. A longer book has 175 more. How many pages in the longer book?
hints: Try drawing a picture or using a place-value chart.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is 425. Work through it place by place.

pretest.question: 'How many more' problems ask you to subtract to find the difference.

feedback.incorrect: The answer is True.

hints: Compare with subtraction — bigger minus smaller.

pretest.question: A farm has 1,250 chickens. Another farm has 480 fewer. How many does the other farm have?

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is A. 1,250−480=770.

hints: Read the problem twice. Identify what's given and what's asked, then choose an operation.

learn.title: Comparison Problems
learn.content: Comparison problems ask how two amounts relate: which is bigger, how much more, or how much less. Using a bar model helps you see the parts.
learn.tts: Comparison problems ask how two amounts relate: which is bigger, how much more, or how much less. Using a bar model helps you see the parts.

learn.title: Draw Two Bars
learn.content: Draw two bars, one for each quantity. Align them at the left. The difference shows at the right end of the longer bar.
learn.tts: Draw two bars, one for each quantity. Align them at the left. The difference shows at the right end of the longer bar.

learn.title: 'More Than' = Add
learn.content: If one amount is 'more than' another, add the difference to the smaller amount to find the larger one.
learn.tts: If one amount is 'more than' another, add the difference to the smaller amount to find the larger one.

learn.title: 'Less Than' = Subtract
learn.content: If one amount is 'less than' another, subtract the difference from the larger amount.
learn.tts: If one amount is 'less than' another, subtract the difference from the larger amount.

learn.title: Find the Difference
learn.content: To find 'how many more' or 'how many fewer,' subtract the smaller amount from the larger amount.
learn.tts: To find 'how many more' or 'how many fewer,' subtract the smaller amount from the larger amount.

learn.title: Label Everything
learn.content: Always label your bars with quantities. Check your answer against the words in the problem.
learn.tts: Always label your bars with quantities. Check your answer against the words in the problem.
